name: Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail
name_original: Circular a la Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes — desde Cerro Helecho
description: This moderate 5.1-mile hike connects Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega, offering green spaces and viewpoints in San Sebastián de los Reyes.
region_intro: Hiking around San Sebastián De Los Reyes, located north of Madrid, offers access to a diverse natural environment. The region features two distinct topographical zones: a western area with moderate slopes and oak forests, and a flatter eastern zone shaped by the Jarama River. Significant portions of the municipality, including the Dehesa Boyal and Monte Coto Pesadilla, are integrated into the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares and a Biosphere Reserve. This area is characterized by holm oak forests, river valleys, and well-maintained trails. Best hiking trails in San Sebastián De Los Reyes The most popular hiking route is Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route connects Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega, offering green spaces and viewpoints. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dehesa Boyal Loop Trail, an easy 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path. This circular route traverses Mediterranean forests within the Dehesa Boyal, providing views and opportunities to observe wildlife. Local hikers also love the Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián y arroyo de Viñuelas — circular desde Polideportivo, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through the Dehesa Boyal and along the Arroyo de Viñuelas, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around San Sebastián De Los Reyes is defined by holm oak forests, river valleys, and varied topography, with options for different ability levels from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in San Sebastián De Los Reyes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore San Sebastián De Los Reyes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails in San Sebastián De Los Reyes?
A: The region offers diverse topography. You'll find a western area with more pronounced reliefs and moderate slopes, characterized by oak forests and streams. The eastern zone, shaped by the Jarama River, is generally flatter. Many trails, especially within the Dehesa Boyal, traverse Mediterranean holm oak forests.

Q: Are there many easy hiking options available in San Sebastián De Los Reyes?
A: Yes, San Sebastián De Los Reyes is very welcoming for hikers of all levels. Out of over 70 available routes, more than 40 are classified as easy, making them suitable for casual strolls or family outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284280579/dehesa-boyal-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Boyal Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0-mile path through Mediterranean forests.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in San Sebastián De Los Reyes are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. The Dehesa Boyal, a significant natural space, features several such routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287086193/dehesa-boyal-de-san-sebastian-y-arroyo-de-vinuelas-circular-desde-polideportivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián y arroyo de Viñuelas — circular desde Polideportivo&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.9-mile trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: San Sebastián De Los Reyes boasts several interesting natural features. Within the Dehesa Boyal, you can discover watering holes, remnants of its grazing past, and even an insect hotel. For panoramic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt;, which offers vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The area also features the Jarama and Guadalix rivers, and smaller streams like Arroyo de Viñuelas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls in the vicinity. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular natural attractions in the region.

Q: Is San Sebastián De Los Reyes a good place for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, with a large number of easy trails and protected natural spaces like the Dehesa Boyal, San Sebastián De Los Reyes is well-suited for family hikes. These routes often feature gentle slopes and interesting points like watering holes, providing an enjoyable experience for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286841565/the-watering-holes-of-the-dehesa-boyal-of-san-sebastian-de-los-reyes-circular-from-polideportivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;watering holes of the Dehesa Boyal&lt;/a&gt; trail is an easy option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Sebastián De Los Reyes?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Even in winter, many trails remain accessible, especially the lower-elevation paths. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: How accessible are the hiking trails by public transport?
A: San Sebastián De Los Reyes is located north of Madrid and is generally well-connected. Many trailheads, particularly those around the Dehesa Boyal, can be reached via local bus services or are within reasonable distance from metro stations, making them accessible without a car.

Q: Are there designated parking areas for hikers?
A: Yes, for popular hiking areas like the Dehesa Boyal, you will typically find designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific trailhead information for the most convenient parking options, especially near recreational areas or sports complexes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Sebastián De Los Reyes?
A: The hiking routes in San Sebastián De Los Reyes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the beauty of the holm oak forests, and the opportunities to observe local wildlife within the protected natural areas.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, some offer a longer distance and slightly more elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287103067/matapinonera-to-arroyo-de-la-vega-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.1-mile hike that provides green spaces and viewpoints, suitable for those looking for a bit more of a workout.
tips: A beautiful viewpoint in the form of a pergola, located on Cerro Helecho, where you can see a panoramic view of Madrid to the South and the Guadarrama mountain range to the North.
Path that surrounds the Dehesa Boyal and enters the plan of its regeneration. It has informative signs and a curious hotel for insects.

faqs.question: Where does the Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail start?
faqs.answer: This circular trail typically starts and ends near the Cerro Helecho area, which is part of the Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes. Specific parking information is not provided, but you can often find street parking in the residential areas adjacent to the Dehesa Boyal or designated parking within the park itself.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail covers a moderate distance of approximately 8.3 km with a gentle elevation gain of about 102 meters. Given its location connecting Matapiñonera and Arroyo de la Vega, the terrain is likely a mix of urban paths, green spaces, and possibly some unpaved sections within the Dehesa Boyal. It's generally accessible for a moderate hike.

faqs.question: Is the Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through residential areas, near wildlife, or within designated park sections like the Dehesa Boyal, where specific regulations might apply. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter a few notable points of interest. You can enjoy views from the Cerro Helecho Viewpoint and pass through the Arco Verde Pollinator Reserve, offering opportunities to appreciate local flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Dehesa Boyal?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail or the Dehesa Boyal de San Sebastián de los Reyes. It's a public access area, but always respect local regulations and signage regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the natural surroundings are at their most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon. Winter can also be pleasant, though cooler.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the "Cordel de la Matapiñonera al Arroyo de la Vega," the "Arco Verde," and the "Senda a fuente de Manao" during your hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, relatively short distance, and gentle elevation, this trail is generally suitable for families with children. The mix of green spaces and urban paths makes it an accessible option for a family outing. Always consider your children's fitness levels and bring plenty of water and snacks.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: While specific public transport stops directly at the trailhead are not detailed, the trail is located near residential areas of San Sebastián de los Reyes and Alcobendas. You can likely reach the vicinity by local bus services, followed by a short walk to the starting point. Check local bus routes for the Dehesa Boyal or Cerro Helecho area.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail connects areas like Matapiñonera and Arroyo de la Vega, which are close to urban centers like San Sebastián de los Reyes and Alcobendas, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops within a short distance of the trail's start and end points, perfect for a post-hike refreshment.

faqs.question: Is the Matapiñonera to Arroyo de la Vega Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it's a loop trail. You will start and finish in approximately the same location, offering a varied experience without retracing your steps.
